servinfo.title: Inspection of ECM and Its Circuits

item.ptxt: As each terminal voltage is affected by battery voltage, confirm that it is 11 V or more when ignition is “ON”.
Voltage with asterisk (*) cannot be measured with voltmeter because it is pulse signal. Use oscilloscope for its check.
Before performing this inspection, be sure to read “Precautions for ECM Circuit Inspection” under Precautions for Engine .

ptxt.intxref: Fuel injector No.4 signal and ignition coil No.4 signal

entry.ptxt: MAP sensor power supply circuit and MAF sensor reference voltage circuit

entry.ptxt: Generator field coil measured signal circuit

entry.ptxt: CAN (high) communication circuit to ABS control module
CAN (low) communication circuit to ABS control module
Electric load current sensor power supply circuit
CPP No.2 switch signal circuit (M/T model)
Electric load current sensor ground circuit
A/C refrigerant pressure sensor ground circuit
Electric load current sensor signal circuit
Transmission range sensor signal circuit (CVT model)
Select lever: Other than “P” or “N” position
Brake booster switch signal circuit (CVT model)
Brake pedal: Fully depressed after depressing several times
A/C refrigerant pressure sensor power supply circuit
A/C refrigerant pressure sensor signal circuit
Radiator cooling fan relay No.2 and No.3 drive circuit
Radiator cooling fan relay No.1 drive circuit
Electric load current sensor signal circuit (battery temperature signal)
Temperature in the vicinity of battery: 25 °C (77 °F)
Temperature in the vicinity of battery: 90 °C (194 °F)

item.ptxt: Display includes the following types of data:
Waveform may vary with measurement condition and vehicle specifications.
